title: Phlomis tenorei
what_description: Phlomis tenorei is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It has a woody base and a branched stem, typically growing up to 1 meter in height. The leaves are opposite, lance-shaped, and have a ciliate margin, measuring 5-10 cm in length. The flowers are purple, tubular, and arranged in a terminal inflorescence, forming a spike-like structure, and are pollinated by bees and other insects. Phlomis tenorei is native to the Mediterranean region and is commonly found in dry, rocky areas and scrublands.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Phlomis tenorei are inflammation, which it reduces due to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, wound healing, facilitated by its antimicrobial properties and ability to promote tissue repair, diarrhea, alleviated by its antidiarrheal properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria. Additionally, it exhibits antibacterial properties, useful in treating gastrointestinal issues, such as gastritis, and gastroenteritis, by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract. Phlomis tenorei also demonstrates antifungal properties, effective against fungal infections, and antifungal properties that combat fungal overgrowth, as well as antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and mitigate oxidative stress, which can contribute to skin irritation and allergic reactions. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties provide pain relief by reducing inflammation and combating the growth of bacteria that cause pain, and its antioxidant properties help alleviate fever by reducing oxidative stress, while also alleviating anxiety by regulating the nervous system. Phlomis tenorei's antimicrobial properties also exhibit antifungal and antioxidant properties that combat fungal infections, hypertension, and rheumatism by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Phlomis tenorei are its antioxidant activity, which helps protect cells from oxidative damage, and its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and volatile oils, contribute to its antimicrobial effects, inhibiting the growth of microorganisms that can cause infections. Its antispasmodic activity helps to relax muscles and alleviate cramps, making it a potential treatment for various musculoskeletal disorders. The analgesic properties of Phlomis tenorei are also noteworthy, as they provide relief from pain without the use of opioids. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, are responsible for these medicinal properties, which have been observed in various studies.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Phlomis tenorei are phlomisoside, a phenylethanoid glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, luteolin and apigenin, two flavonoids with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that have shown potential in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Additionally, the plant contains rosmarinic acid, a diterpene carboxylic acid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Furthermore, Phlomis tenorei is rich in flavonoids, which are a class of compounds known to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities that can help protect against diseases. The combination of these bioactive compounds may contribute to the plant's ability to exhibit a range of pharmacological effects, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. These constituents have been identified through various studies that have isolated and characterized the bioactive compounds present in Phlomis tenorei.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Phlomis tenorei are infusion, tea, tincture, decoction and salve. Infusion is a liquid extract of dried flowers, leaves and stems steeped in hot water for 5-10 minutes, while tea is a stronger liquid made by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried material in boiling water for 5-10 minutes. Tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of Phlomis tenorei made by soaking the plant material in a solvent, usually ethanol or glycerin, and then straining and diluting it with water. Decoction is a liquid extract made by boiling the plant material in water for 10-20 minutes to release its active compounds. Salve is a topical ointment made from the plant's leaves, flowers and stems infused in oil, and used to treat skin conditions such as wounds, cuts and eczema.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Phlomis tenorei improperly include inducing vomiting due to the presence of anthraquinones which can cause irritation to the digestive tract. Misuse of Phlomis tenorei may also cause diarrhea due to the cathartic effect of the anthraquinones on the intestinal muscles, leading to excessive water loss and electrolyte imbalance. Moreover, Phlomis tenorei contains allergenic compounds that can trigger allergic reactions in sensitive individuals, such as skin rashes, hives, and itching. Ingestion of excessive amounts of Phlomis tenorei can lead to an increased heart rate due to the presence of stimulant compounds that affect the nervous system, potentially causing arrhythmias and tachycardia. An overdose of Phlomis tenorei can result in serious health complications, including cardiac arrest, respiratory failure, and even death, making proper use and dosing crucial to avoid these adverse effects.
uses: Phlomis tenorei has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. It is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some regions, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and reduce fever. The leaves and flowers of Phlomis tenorei are often used to prepare herbal remedies. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its medicinal applications.
taxonomy: Phlomis tenorei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Phlomis, which consists of various species of shrubs and perennials native to the Mediterranean region. The Lamiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as mint or deadnettle family. Phlomis tenorei is native to the island of Sicily, where it grows in dry, rocky areas and woodlands. Within the Lamiaceae family, Phlomis tenorei is often characterized by its distinctive, yellow flowers and shrubby growth habit.
benefits: Phlomis tenorei, also known as Tenore's phlomis, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that Phlomis tenorei may have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various infections. More research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Phlomis tenorei.
